decision.choice: Expand presets from model-role mappings into partial operating profiles that may also declare orchestration.profile.
decision.rationale: Model choice and preferred coordination strategy are related deployment decisions. Built-in presets can make the intended behavior explicit without brittle runtime intelligence heuristics.

alternative.name: Hard-coded model ID matching
alternative.reason_rejected: Model aliases and capability assumptions change frequently, and hidden matching would make behavior difficult to predict.

alternative.name: Model roles only
alternative.reason_rejected: Roles select models but cannot express whether the controller should work directly or orchestrate subagents.

decision.choice: Keep one vv-controller and resolve one concrete system policy at runtime from the startup config snapshot.
decision.rationale: A single controller avoids duplicated prompts, managed-agent proliferation, workflow tool identity changes, and drift. Runtime resolution supports existing config layers while exposing only the selected instruction to the model.

alternative.name: Separate single-session, balanced, and orchestrated primary agents
alternative.reason_rejected: Multiple agents would duplicate prompts, expand registration state, complicate default-agent switching, and require WorkflowPlugin access changes.

alternative.name: Generate profile-specific prompt files during sync
alternative.reason_rejected: Sync-time generation complicates global/project scope alignment and can leave prompt files stale relative to effective config.

decision.choice: Inject only one concrete policy and remove inactive orchestration branches from ordinary vv-controller context.
decision.rationale: The feature exists to prevent context pollution and avoid making a high-capability model reason about optional coordination mechanisms that are not active.

alternative.name: Describe all profiles and identify the active one
alternative.reason_rejected: The inactive alternatives still consume context and invite the model to reinterpret or bypass the configured policy.

decision.choice: Keep exploration, investigation, planning, implementation, and verification in the primary session while permitting reviewer subagents for explicit or materially valuable independent evaluation.
decision.rationale: Delegated understanding creates lossy handoffs, but independent review benefits from a fresh context and a different model. The primary remains responsible for validating findings. It fixes confirmed findings directly when review belongs to active implementation, while explicit review-only requests remain findings-only until the user asks for fixes.

alternative.name: Prohibit every subagent including reviewers
alternative.reason_rejected: It removes useful cross-model and independent evaluation.

alternative.name: Mandatory reviewers after every non-trivial change
alternative.reason_rejected: It recreates the latency and cost problem for routine work.

decision.choice: Use prompt-only orchestration behavior in the first version.
decision.rationale: This is the smallest design that tests whether concrete profile prompts deliver the intended behavior without adding tool-state machinery or conflicting with explicit vv-execute classic mode.

alternative.name: Disable or filter task and workflow tools
alternative.reason_rejected: Hard gating would require exceptions and runtime state for vv-execute classic, increasing complexity before prompt behavior is measured.

alternative.name: Session-scoped runtime overrides
alternative.reason_rejected: The user explicitly preferred prompt semantics over a second state machine.

decision.choice: Retain explicit inline/classic choice inside vv-execute and let that specialized skill prompt override ordinary preset behavior for the selected plan execution.
decision.rationale: The user intentionally added inline mode but still wants classic available as a deliberate workflow choice. Optionality is acceptable after explicit skill invocation, not in ordinary controller context.

alternative.name: Derive vv-execute mode automatically from orchestration profile
alternative.reason_rejected: It removes an intentional user decision and makes classic execution inaccessible from single-session presets.

decision.choice: Add optional orchestration fields to schema v3 and resolve missing root configuration to balanced.
decision.rationale: The addition can be backward compatible. Existing valid configs should continue operating without a forced schema migration, while explicit invalid values still fail closed.

alternative.name: Required schema v4 section
alternative.reason_rejected: It would impose migration cost without adding necessary expressiveness or safety.

assumption.statement: Concrete prompt instructions are sufficient to evaluate the value of orchestration profiles before adding harness enforcement.
assumption.fragile_if: Configured models repeatedly ignore single-session instructions and invoke working subagents despite clear prompts.

assumption.statement: Independent cross-model review provides enough value to remain available in single-session mode.
assumption.fragile_if: Measured reviewer findings are consistently lower quality than direct primary review or produce excessive false positives and rework.

assumption.statement: The existing startup config snapshot is the correct lifecycle boundary for orchestration policy.
assumption.fragile_if: OpenCode introduces a supported config hot-reload lifecycle that guarantees stable per-session policies.

item.decision: Whether to enforce profiles by disabling tools, restricting task targets, or adding permissions.
item.why_deferred: Prompt-only behavior should be observed before adding state and enforcement complexity.
item.revisit_trigger: Repeated policy violations or material context/tool-schema overhead caused by unused task capabilities.

item.decision: Whether to add cost, latency, token, and cache effectiveness telemetry by profile.
item.why_deferred: The initial feature is behavioral and configuration-focused.
item.revisit_trigger: Need for quantitative preset tuning or disagreement about profile effectiveness.

item.decision: Whether built-in preset profile mappings should change as model capabilities evolve.
item.why_deferred: The initial mapping reflects current intended usage and can be updated declaratively later.
item.revisit_trigger: A preset changes its primary smart model or observed behavior no longer matches its assigned profile.

scenario.name: High-capability ordinary implementation
scenario.context: vv-osovv sets single-session and vv-controller receives a multi-file implementation request.
scenario.implications: The controller reads, reasons, edits, and verifies directly. It does not receive inactive implementation-loop instructions and does not delegate working context.

scenario.name: Risk-driven independent review
scenario.context: A single-session implementation materially affects public config or another risky surface.
scenario.implications: The controller may launch reviewer subagents for one independent round, validates findings itself, applies confirmed fixes directly, and verifies without dispatching an implementer.

scenario.name: Explicit classic plan execution
scenario.context: The active preset is single-session, but the user invokes vv-execute and selects classic.
scenario.implications: The loaded skill prompt authorizes classic tracked execution for that plan without modifying runtime orchestration state.

scenario.name: Existing schema v3 installation
scenario.context: An existing valid config has no orchestration section.
scenario.implications: The config remains valid and resolves to balanced until a built-in preset or orchestration set command writes an explicit profile.

scenario.name: Partial custom preset
scenario.context: A user-defined preset changes model roles but omits orchestration.
scenario.implications: The current root profile remains unchanged and CLI output makes that fact visible.

constraint.impact: Profile resolution and concrete system injection occur from the startup config snapshot; config changes require restart.
Existing invalid configs fail loudly, writes remain conservative and atomic, and valid v3 documents are not silently migrated to a new version.
Reviewer launches continue using existing work-item identity, strict result parsing, aggregation, persistence, and hard-stop semantics.
